checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 3 (image_index 3) shows the Alarms directory visible within the sdk_gphone64_arm64 storage area, which matches the evidence hint for the checkpoint.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 5 (image_index 4) shows the Alarms directory contents with the 'Images' filter applied, displaying the sunset.jpg file. Step 6's screenshot further confirms the file is present by showing it selected in the Alarms directory.

Step 4 opens the Alarms directory, and step 5 applies the Images filter to display image files, successfully revealing sunset.jpg in the Alarms directory, which provides the required screenshot evidence confirming the file is present in Alarms.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 6 confirms sunset.jpg is selected (marked with a checkmark, '1 selected' text shown); screenshot from step7 displays the 'Move to...' option in the menu, and step8's action of clicking 'Move to...' initiates the move process, leading to the destination selection screen (step9). This fulfills the checkpoint requirement of selecting the file and choosing the 'Move' option to start the move action.

Step 7 opens the additional options menu to reveal the 'Move to…' option. Step 8 selects the 'Move to…' option, which directly initiates the move action for sunset.jpg, matching the checkpoint's requirement of selecting the file and choosing the 'Move' option from the menu.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 10's screenshot confirms the Pictures directory exists within sdk_gphone64_arm64 storage; step 12's screenshot shows a 'Moving 1 item' snackbar indicating the move operation was initiated.

checkpoint_results.evidence: The final screenshot (step15) shows sunset.jpg still present in the Alarms folder, with no visible "Pasted" notification or confirmation prompt indicating the move completed successfully, and no verification of the file appearing in the Pictures directory.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 5 (image_index 3) shows the event's title field containing the exact text 'Appointment for Annual Report', and screenshot from step 12 (image_index 4) confirms the title is present in the event form before saving. The event was saved in step 13, and step 14's calendar view displays the event on 2023-10-15 (truncated as 'Appointm', consistent with the full title).
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 3 opens the event creation interface where the title field is accessible. Step 4 directly inputs the exact required title 'Appointment for Annual Report' into the title field, with the summary confirming the field now contains this text. Step 13 saves the event, transitioning to the calendar view where the event with the specified title is displayed. Step 14 confirms the task completion, indicating the title was successfully set and saved. These steps provide direct and supporting evidence that the event has the exact required title.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 5 shows the exact description text 'We will discuss annual budget.' entered in the event's description field; screenshot from step 12 confirms this description remains in the event form before saving, and the event was successfully saved in step 13.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step 5 directly shows the exact description text 'We will discuss annual budget.' being entered and successfully populated in the event's description field, matching the evidence hint's requirement for a screenshot of the description field with the text. Step 13 confirms the event was saved with all entered details, including the description. Step 14 verifies the event creation process was completed successfully, ensuring the description is part of the saved calendar event.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step 4's screenshot shows the event creation screen with start date 'October 15 (Sun)' (which is 2023-10-15, as 2023-10-15 was a Sunday). Step 7's screenshot confirms the start time was set to 15:00 via the time picker. Step 14's calendar view shows the event displayed on October 15, matching the required date and time.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Steps 6-8 demonstrate the agent successfully setting and confirming the event's start time to 15:00, fulfilling the time requirement of the checkpoint. Step3 opens the event creation interface where the date field should be configured, but no explicit action to set the date to 2023-10-15 is shown. Steps13-14 show the event being saved and the agent marking the task as complete, but there is no direct evidence the event is scheduled on 2023-10-15.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_fallback_reason: No steps provide conclusive evidence that the event's start date was set to 2023-10-15; the date component of the checkpoint is not verified, only the start time is confirmed.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Screenshot from step 12 shows the event's start time as 15:00 and end time as 15:15 on 2023-10-15, confirming a 15-minute duration; the event is visible in the calendar view (step14) on that date.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: These steps show the event's start time being set to 15:00, end time adjusted to 15:15 (creating a 15-minute duration), confirmation of the time settings, saving the event, and the final calendar view where the event should span from 15:00 to 15:15, directly supporting the checkpoint's requirement of a 15-minute event ending at 15:15 on 2023-10-15.

checkpoint_results.evidence: Step12's screenshot confirms the event has the correct title, description, date (2023-10-15), start time (15:00), and end time (15:15, matching the 15-minute duration). Step13's screenshot shows the event is being saved (with a 'Saving...' indicator) and a notification displays the full event details, while the calendar view shows a truncated entry 'Appointm' under October 15. Step14's screenshot confirms the event entry remains visible on October 15 in the calendar view, verifying the event is successfully saved and appears in the required calendar view.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_rationale: Step13 records that the event was saved and the app transitioned to the main calendar view; step14 shows the agent marked the task as complete. These steps relate to the event being saved, but they do not provide evidence that the event is set to the required date (2023-10-15) or visible in that date's calendar view.
checkpoint_results.retrieval_fallback_reason: The trace contains no steps where the event date was set to 2023-10-15, and no screenshot confirms the event appears in the calendar view for that specific date. This means there is insufficient evidence to verify the checkpoint requirement that the event is visible in the calendar view for 2023-10-15.
